message: Calcium inhibits the absorption of Iron. It is recommended to separate their intake by at least 2 hours.
High doses of Zinc compete with Copper absorption, which may lead to Copper deficiency. Consider taking them at different times.
Vitamin K conflicts with anticoagulant medications (blood thinners like Warfarin) by promoting clotting. Consult your physician.
Caffeine counteracts the sleep-inducing effects of Melatonin. Avoid caffeine close to taking Melatonin.
Green tea extract (EGCG) can bind to Iron, significantly reducing its absorption. Take them at different times of the day.
